title: astracantha adanica
intro: Astracantha adanica, also known as the wild rue or Persian rue, is a perennial herb native to the Middle East and Central Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic effects, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids such as berberine and skimmianine. The health benefits of Astracantha adanica include relief from digestive issues, such as nausea and indigestion, as well as its use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Astracantha adanica can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and stems are often dried and used in herbal remedies to treat a range of ailments.
